title: A Functionally Enriched Form of Dap as an Earthcube Building Block
author: Fulker, Dave
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This presentation was given at the 2014 OPeNDAP Developers Workshop that was held from 7-8 July 2014. The intent of the workshop was to demonstrate and discuss diverse efforts related to the Data Access Protocol (DAP), with a special interest in how DAP and its applications were evolving. 
  Summary of presentation:  
 The ODSIP (Open Data-Service Invocation Protocol) project, which is funded by NSF, is a functionally enriched form of DAP (Data Access Protocol) that serves as an EarthCube building block. Foundational concepts from DODS (now OpenDAP) circa 1994 are discussed in this presentation, highlighting URL-based dataset retrieval and subset creation capabilities. 
 The ODSIP project aims to address largely unmet data-retrieval needs by supporting richer subsetting, additional operations, and invoking server-side functions. Under DAP4, the stage is set for ODSIP, with clearer descriptions, hooks for extensibility, and prototype subsets of UGRIDS. 
 Key features of DAP4 include URL-based subset expressions and function expressions enabling server-specific extensions. The ODSIP project, funded by NSF, seeks to develop a service-invocation protocol supporting rich pre-retrieval processing and prototype its use in demanding geographic contexts like climate-model downscaling and storm surge prediction. 
 In conclusion, ODSIP extends the OPeNDAP protocol, supports rich pre-retrieval processing, and aims to engage the EarthCube community while demonstrating its utility in diverse geographic contexts.
